messages.content: Draft a lease agreement for a 2BHK flat in Bandra, Mumbai for 11 months. Rent is 50k. Use the Auto-Draft tool.

# LEASE AGREEMENT

This Lease Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is made and entered into on this ______ day of __________, 20____ at Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.

---

## BETWEEN

**LANDLORD:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Phone: _________________________
Aadhaar/PAN: _________________________

(hereinafter referred to as the "Owner/Landlord", which expression shall unless excluded by or repugnant to the context include his heirs, legal representatives, and assigns)

---

## AND

**TENANT:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Phone: _________________________
Aadhaar/PAN: _________________________

(hereinafter referred to as the "Tenant", which expression shall unless excluded by or repugnant to the context include his heirs, legal representatives, and permitted assigns)

---

## PROPERTY DETAILS

**Premises:** 2 BEDROOM HALL KITCHEN (2BHK) FLAT

**Address:** _______________________________________________________
Bandra, Mumbai, Maharashtra - ___________

**Furnishing Status:** Unfurnished / Partially Furnished / Fully Furnished

---

## 1. TERM OF LEASE

1.1 This Agreement is for a period of **ELEVEN (11) MONTHS** commencing from __________ and ending on __________.

1.2 The Tenancy shall be renewable on mutual agreement of both parties.

---

## 2. RENT

2.1 The monthly rent shall be **Rs. 50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand Only)**.

2.2 Rent shall be paid on or before the **5th day of each calendar month**.

2.3 Payment Mode: Bank Transfer / Cheque / Cash (specify) _____________

2.4 Rent Escalation: No increase during the lease term / _____% increase on renewal (specify).

---

## 3. SECURITY DEPOSIT

3.1 The Tenant has paid a sum of Rs. _____________/- (Rupees ________________ Only) as Security Deposit/Interest Free Deposit with the Landlord.

3.2 This deposit shall be refunded to the Tenant at the time of vacating the premises, after deducting any outstanding dues, repairs, or damages as per this Agreement.

3.3 The deposit shall be refunded within _____ days of vacating the premises.

---

## 4. MAINTENANCE & UTILITIES

4.1 **Maintenance Charges:** Rs. _____ per month (if applicable).

4.2 **Tenant's Responsibility:** The Tenant shall be solely responsible for payment of:
- Electricity charges
- Water charges
- Gas/LPG bills
- Internet/Wi-Fi bills
- Telephone bills
- Any other utility bills consumed by the Tenant

4.3 **Owner's Responsibility:** The Landlord shall be responsible for:
- Property tax
- Building maintenance (common areas)
- Structural repairs

---

## 5. PARKING

5.1 Parking Facility: Provided / Not Provided (delete as applicable)

5.2 If provided: _______ Two-wheeler / Four-wheeler parking space(s).

5.3 Parking Charges: Rs. _____ per month (if applicable).

---

## 6. PERMITTED USE

6.1 The premises shall be used only for **residential purposes**.

6.2 The Tenant shall not use the premises for commercial activities, running a business, or any illegal purpose.

---

## 7. PETS

7.1 Pets Allowed: Yes / No

7.2 If allowed: Only _____ pet(s) of non-dangerous breed.

7.3 Pet deposit (if applicable): Rs. _____.

---

## 8. SUB-LETTING & TRANSFER

8.1 The Tenant shall not sub-let, assign, or transfer the premises or any part thereof to any third party without prior written consent of the Landlord.

---

## 9. REPAIRS & MAINTENANCE

9.1 **Tenant's Responsibilities:**
- Keep the premises in good and tenantable condition
- Report any damages or repairs required to the Landlord within 48 hours
- Minor repairs upto Rs. _____ shall be borne by the Tenant

9.2 **Landlord's Responsibilities:**
- Structural repairs and major maintenance
- Repairs affecting essential services (plumbing, electrical main lines)

---

## 10. LOCK-IN PERIOD

10.1 Either party wishing to terminate this Agreement before the expiry of the lock-in period of _____ months shall pay _____ months' rent as penalty to the other party.

---

## 11. NOTICE PERIOD

11.1 Either party wishing to terminate this Agreement at the end of the lease term or thereafter shall give _____ month(s) prior written notice to the other party.

11.2 If the Tenant vacates before the expiry of the lease term without proper notice, the security deposit may be forfeited.

---

## 12. INSPECTION

12.1 The Landlord shall have the right to inspect the premises with prior intimation of at least 24 hours, during reasonable hours.

---

## 13. RIGHTS & OBLIGATIONS

**Landlord's Rights:**
- Receive rent punctually
- Inspect the property with prior notice
- Terminate agreement on breach of terms

**Tenant's Rights:**
- Peaceful enjoyment of the premises
- Privacy
- Receive advance notice before inspections

**Tenant's Obligations:**
- Pay rent on time
- Maintain the property in good condition
- Not cause nuisance to neighbors
- Follow building society rules

---

## 14. TERMINATION

14.1 This Agreement shall stand terminated:
- On expiry of the lease period
- On serving of proper notice as per Clause 11
- On breach of any terms of this Agreement

14.2 On termination, the Tenant shall vacate the premises and hand over vacant possession to the Landlord in the same condition as at the time of taking possession (normal wear and tear excepted).

---

## 15. FORCE MAJEURE

15.1 Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing their obligations under this Agreement due to circumstances beyond their reasonable control (natural disasters, war, pandemic, etc.).

---

## 16. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

16.1 Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be resolved through mutual discussion.

16.2 If unresolved, the dispute shall be subject to the jurisdiction of courts in **Mumbai, Maharashtra**.

---

## 17. GOVERNING LAW

17.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India, specifically the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999 and the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

---

## 18. MISCELLANEOUS

18.1 **Amendments:** Any amendment to this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.

18.2 **Entire Agreement:** This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties.

18.3 **Severability:** If any provision is found invalid, the remaining provisions shall continue in force.

18.4 **Notices:** All notices shall be in writing and delivered personally or by registered post to the addresses mentioned above.

---

## 19. LIST OF ANNEXURES/ADDENDUM

- [ ] Rent Receipts
- [ ] Inventory List
- [ ] Property Photographs
- [ ] ID Proofs of Both Parties
- [ ] Address Proofs

---

## SIGNATURES

**LANDLORD:**

_________________________
Name: _________________________
Date: _________________________

**TENANT:**

_________________________
Name: _________________________
Date: _________________________

---

## WITNESSES

**Witness 1:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Signature: _________________________

**Witness 2:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Signature: _________________________

---

*This is a template agreement. It is recommended to consult a qualified advocate before executing the final agreement to ensure compliance with applicable laws and to address specific requirements.*

*For registration of this agreement, please check with the local sub-registrar office regarding the requirement for registration under the Registration Act, 1908.*

RTI Online :: Guidelines for use of RTI Online Portal
GUIDELINES FOR USE OF RTI ONLINE PORTAL
This Web Portal can be used by Indian citizens to file RTI application online and also to make payment for RTI application online. First appeal can also be filed online.
An applicant who desires to obtain any information under the RTI Act can make a request through this Web Portal to the Ministries/Departments of Government of India.
On clicking at "Submit Request", the applicant has to fill the required details on the page that will appear.
The fields marked
*
are mandatory while the others are optional.
The text of the application may be written at the prescribed column.
At present, the text of an application that can be uploaded at the prescribed column is confined to 3000 characters only.
Only alphabets A-Z a-z number 0-9 and special characters , . - _ ( ) / @ : & ? \ % are allowed in Text for RTI Request application.
In case an application contains more than 3000 characters, it can be uploaded as an attachment, by using column "Supporting document".
Do not upload Aadhar Card or PAN Card or any other personal Identification (Except BPL Card).
PDF file name should not have any blank spaces.
After filling the first page, the applicant has to click on "Make Payment" to make payment of the prescribed fee.
The applicant can pay the prescribed fee through the following modes:
(a) Internet banking;
(b) Using credit/debit card of Master/Visa;
(c) Using UPI
(d) Using RuPay Card.
Fee for making an application is as prescribed in the RTI Rules, 2012.
After making payment, an application can be submitted.
After making payment, if applicant didn’t receive the registration number then applicant is advised to wait for the 24-48 working hours as registration number will be generated after reconciliation. Please do not make additional attempt to make payment again. If it is not generated within 24-48 hours kindly send an e-mail at helprtionline-dopt[at]nic[dot]in with transaction details.
No RTI fee is required to be paid by any citizen who is below poverty line as per RTI Rules, 2012. However, the applicant must attach a copy of the certificate issued by the appropriate government in this regard, alongwith the application.
On submission of an application, a unique registration number would be issued, which may be referred by the applicant for any references in future.
The application filed through this Web Portal would reach electronically to the "Nodal Officer" of concerned Ministry/Department, who would transmit the RTI application electronically to the concerned CPIO.
In case additional fee is required representing the cost for providing information, the CPIO would intimate the applicant through this portal. This intimation can be seen by the applicant through Status Report or through his/her e-mail alert.
For making an appeal to the first Appellate Authority, the applicant has to click at "Submit First Appeal" and fill up the page that will appear.
The registration number of original application has to be used for reference.
As per RTI Act, no fee has to be paid for first appeal.
The applicant/the appellant should submit his/her mobile number to receive SMS alert.
Status of the RTI application/first appeal filed online can be seen by the applicant/appellant by clicking at "View Status".
All the requirements for filing an RTI application and first appeal as well as other provisions regarding time limit, exemptions etc., as provided in the RTI Act, 2005 will continue to apply.

NO. 25 OF 2005 
[23rd June, 2005.] 
An Act further to amend the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973. 
BE it enacted by Parliament in the Fifty-sixth Year of the Republic of India as follows:— 
1. Short title and commencement. —(1) This Act may be called the Code of Criminal Procedure 
(Amendment) Act, 2005. 
(2) Save as otherwise provided in this Act, it shall come into force on such date as the Central Government 
may, by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint 1[; and different dates* may be appointed for different 
provisions of this Act.] 
* * * * * 
16. Insertion of new section 144A .—In Chapter X of the principal Act, under sub -heading 
“C.—Urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger ”, after section 144, the following section 
shall be inserted, namely: — 
‘144A. Power to prohibit carryi ng arms in procession or mass drill or mass training with 
arms.—(1) The District Magistrate may, whenever he considers it necessary so to do for the 
preservation of public peace or public safety or for the maintenance of public order, by public notice 
or by order, prohibit in any area within the local limits of his jurisdiction, the carrying of arms in any 
procession or the organising or holding of, or taking part in, any mass drill or mass training with arms 
in any public place. 
(2) A public notice issued or an order made under this section may be directed to a particular 
person or to persons belonging to any community, party or organisation. 
(3) No public notice issued or an order made under this section shall remain in force for more 
than three months from the date on which it is issued or made. 
(4) The State Government may, if it considers necessary so to do for the preservation of public 
peace or public safety or for the maintenance of public order, by notification, direct that a public 
notice issued or order made by the District Magistrate under this section shall remain in force for such 
further period not exceeding six months from the date on which such public notice or order was 
issued or made by the District Magistrate would have, but for such direct ion, expired, as it may 
specify in the said notification. 
(5) The State Government may, subject to such control and directions as it may deem fit to 
impose, by general or special order, delegate its powers under sub -section ( 4) to the District 
Magistrate.

# How to File an FIR in India (Specifically for Mumbai, Maharashtra)

## 📋 What is an FIR?
An FIR (First Information Report) is a document filed with the police when a **cognizable offense** has been committed. It sets the criminal investigation process in motion.

---

## 🚨 Quick Emergency Contacts (Mumbai)

| Service | Contact |
|---------|---------|
| **Emergency** | 100 |
| **Women Helpline** | 1091 |
| **Child Helpline** | 1098 |
| **Cyber Crime** | 1930 |
| **Mumbai Police HQ** | +91-22-22621161 |

**Address:** Mumbai Police Headquarters, D.K. Sandhu Marg, Near Mahim Junction, Mumbai - 400016

---

## 📝 Steps to File an FIR

### Step 1: Gather Required Documents
- **Photo ID:** Aadhaar Card/PAN Card/Passport/Voter ID (original + 2 copies)
- **Address Proof:** Electricity bill, bank statement, lease agreement
- **Passport Size Photos:** 3 copies
- **Evidence:** Any documents, photos, screenshots related to the incident
- **Written Complaint:** Can be written on plain paper (2 copies)

### Step 2: Visit the Police Station
- Go to the **police station having jurisdiction** over the area where the incident occurred
- Police stations operate **24/7** for complaints and emergencies

### Step 3: File the Complaint
- You can file your complaint **orally or in writing**
- If written, detail: date, time, place, and facts of the incident
- Police will record your statement in the General Diary/Daily Diary
- **Sign** the complaint after verification

### Step 4: Get Your FIR Copy
- **IMPORTANT:** Under Section 154 CrPC, you MUST get a free copy of the FIR
- Ask for it immediately after the FIR is registered
- Note down: FIR number, Police Station name, Investigating Officer's name & phone

---

## ⚠️ If Police Refuses to Register FIR

If the police refuses to accept your complaint:

1. **Ask for written reason** for refusal
2. **Request a 'Zero FIR'** - can be filed at ANY police station regardless of jurisdiction
3. Say: *"I am hereby requesting that my complaint be registered. If you refuse, please provide a written order stating the reasons for refusal under Section 154(3) of CrPC."*
4. **Escalate** to Circle Inspector, DCP, or CP office
5. **File online** via Mumbai Police portal (mumbaipolice.gov.in) or MahaPolice app
6. **Approach Magistrate** under Section 156(3) CrPC if FIR not registered within 24 hours

---

## 📄 Documents Checklist for FIR Filing

| Document | Copies Required | Notes |
|----------|-----------------|-------|
| Written Complaint | 2 (self-attested) | Detail incident with date, time, place |
| Aadhaar Card | 2 (self-attested) | Primary ID proof |
| Address Proof | 2 (self-attested) | Bill, bank statement, lease agreement |
| Passport Size Photos | 3 | May be needed for acknowledgment |
| Evidence/Documents | 2 (self-attested) | Medical reports, photos, screenshots |
| Witness Details | 1 (if available) | Names, addresses, contact numbers |

**Fee:** ₹0 - No fee is charged for filing an FIR

---

## 🔐 Your Rights When Filing FIR

1. **Right to FIR copy** - Free of cost under Section 154 CrPC
2. **Right to register FIR** - For cognizable offenses, police MUST register
3. **Zero FIR** - Can file at any police station
4. **Online FIR** - Available for certain cases in Maharashtra
5. **Magistrate intervention** - If police fails to act, approach Magistrate under Section 156(3)

---

## 📞 Escalation Authorities (If Needed)

1. Senior Police Inspector of the concerned police station
2. Deputy Commissioner of Police (DCP) of the Zone
3. Commissioner of Police, Mumbai - +91-22-22621161
4. DGP, Maharashtra - +91-222-2021100
5. Maharashtra State Police Complaint Authority
6. Maharashtra Human Rights Commission

---

**Note:** This is general informational guidance about the FIR filing process. For specific legal advice related to your incident, please consult a lawyer.

Do you have a specific incident you'd like to report? If you provide more details (what happened, where, when), I can give you more specific guidance.

This Lease Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement") is made and entered into on this ______ day of __________, 20____ at Mumbai, Maharashtra, India.

---

## BETWEEN

**LANDLORD:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Phone: _________________________
Aadhaar/PAN: _________________________

(hereinafter referred to as the "Owner/Landlord", which expression shall unless excluded by or repugnant to the context include his heirs, legal representatives, and assigns)

---

## AND

**TENANT:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Phone: _________________________
Aadhaar/PAN: _________________________

(hereinafter referred to as the "Tenant", which expression shall unless excluded by or repugnant to the context include his heirs, legal representatives, and permitted assigns)

---

## PROPERTY DETAILS

**Premises:** 2 BEDROOM HALL KITCHEN (2BHK) FLAT

**Address:** _______________________________________________________
Bandra, Mumbai, Maharashtra - ___________

**Furnishing Status:** Unfurnished / Partially Furnished / Fully Furnished

---

## 1. TERM OF LEASE

1.1 This Agreement is for a period of **ELEVEN (11) MONTHS** commencing from __________ and ending on __________.

1.2 The Tenancy shall be renewable on mutual agreement of both parties.

---

## 2. RENT

2.1 The monthly rent shall be **Rs. 50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand Only)**.

2.2 Rent shall be paid on or before the **5th day of each calendar month**.

2.3 Payment Mode: Bank Transfer / Cheque / Cash (specify) _____________

2.4 Rent Escalation: No increase during the lease term / _____% increase on renewal (specify).

---

## 3. SECURITY DEPOSIT

3.1 The Tenant has paid a sum of Rs. _____________/- (Rupees ________________ Only) as Security Deposit/Interest Free Deposit with the Landlord.

3.2 This deposit shall be refunded to the Tenant at the time of vacating the premises, after deducting any outstanding dues, repairs, or damages as per this Agreement.

3.3 The deposit shall be refunded within _____ days of vacating the premises.

---

## 4. MAINTENANCE & UTILITIES

4.1 **Maintenance Charges:** Rs. _____ per month (if applicable).

4.2 **Tenant's Responsibility:** The Tenant shall be solely responsible for payment of:
- Electricity charges
- Water charges
- Gas/LPG bills
- Internet/Wi-Fi bills
- Telephone bills
- Any other utility bills consumed by the Tenant

4.3 **Owner's Responsibility:** The Landlord shall be responsible for:
- Property tax
- Building maintenance (common areas)
- Structural repairs

---

## 5. PARKING

5.1 Parking Facility: Provided / Not Provided (delete as applicable)

5.2 If provided: _______ Two-wheeler / Four-wheeler parking space(s).

5.3 Parking Charges: Rs. _____ per month (if applicable).

---

## 6. PERMITTED USE

6.1 The premises shall be used only for **residential purposes**.

6.2 The Tenant shall not use the premises for commercial activities, running a business, or any illegal purpose.

---

## 7. PETS

7.1 Pets Allowed: Yes / No

7.2 If allowed: Only _____ pet(s) of non-dangerous breed.

7.3 Pet deposit (if applicable): Rs. _____.

---

## 8. SUB-LETTING & TRANSFER

8.1 The Tenant shall not sub-let, assign, or transfer the premises or any part thereof to any third party without prior written consent of the Landlord.

---

## 9. REPAIRS & MAINTENANCE

9.1 **Tenant's Responsibilities:**
- Keep the premises in good and tenantable condition
- Report any damages or repairs required to the Landlord within 48 hours
- Minor repairs upto Rs. _____ shall be borne by the Tenant

9.2 **Landlord's Responsibilities:**
- Structural repairs and major maintenance
- Repairs affecting essential services (plumbing, electrical main lines)

---

## 10. LOCK-IN PERIOD

10.1 Either party wishing to terminate this Agreement before the expiry of the lock-in period of _____ months shall pay _____ months' rent as penalty to the other party.

---

## 11. NOTICE PERIOD

11.1 Either party wishing to terminate this Agreement at the end of the lease term or thereafter shall give _____ month(s) prior written notice to the other party.

11.2 If the Tenant vacates before the expiry of the lease term without proper notice, the security deposit may be forfeited.

---

## 12. INSPECTION

12.1 The Landlord shall have the right to inspect the premises with prior intimation of at least 24 hours, during reasonable hours.

---

## 13. RIGHTS & OBLIGATIONS

**Landlord's Rights:**
- Receive rent punctually
- Inspect the property with prior notice
- Terminate agreement on breach of terms

**Tenant's Rights:**
- Peaceful enjoyment of the premises
- Privacy
- Receive advance notice before inspections

**Tenant's Obligations:**
- Pay rent on time
- Maintain the property in good condition
- Not cause nuisance to neighbors
- Follow building society rules

---

## 14. TERMINATION

14.1 This Agreement shall stand terminated:
- On expiry of the lease period
- On serving of proper notice as per Clause 11
- On breach of any terms of this Agreement

14.2 On termination, the Tenant shall vacate the premises and hand over vacant possession to the Landlord in the same condition as at the time of taking possession (normal wear and tear excepted).

---

## 15. FORCE MAJEURE

15.1 Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performing their obligations under this Agreement due to circumstances beyond their reasonable control (natural disasters, war, pandemic, etc.).

---

## 16. DISPUTE RESOLUTION

16.1 Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be resolved through mutual discussion.

16.2 If unresolved, the dispute shall be subject to the jurisdiction of courts in **Mumbai, Maharashtra**.

---

## 17. GOVERNING LAW

17.1 This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of India, specifically the Maharashtra Rent Control Act, 1999 and the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

---

## 18. MISCELLANEOUS

18.1 **Amendments:** Any amendment to this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.

18.2 **Entire Agreement:** This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties.

18.3 **Severability:** If any provision is found invalid, the remaining provisions shall continue in force.

18.4 **Notices:** All notices shall be in writing and delivered personally or by registered post to the addresses mentioned above.

---

## 19. LIST OF ANNEXURES/ADDENDUM

- [ ] Rent Receipts
- [ ] Inventory List
- [ ] Property Photographs
- [ ] ID Proofs of Both Parties
- [ ] Address Proofs

---

## SIGNATURES

**LANDLORD:**

_________________________
Name: _________________________
Date: _________________________

**TENANT:**

_________________________
Name: _________________________
Date: _________________________

---

## WITNESSES

**Witness 1:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Signature: _________________________

**Witness 2:**
Name: _________________________
Address: _________________________
Signature: _________________________

---

*This is a template agreement. It is recommended to consult a qualified advocate before executing the final agreement to ensure compliance with applicable laws and to address specific requirements.*

*For registration of this agreement, please check with the local sub-registrar office regarding the requirement for registration under the Registration Act, 1908.*""")"
